Description: Iraqi forces invade Kuwait, triggering the Gulf War and fears of a major supply disruption from the Persian Gulf.
US-led coalition launches the air campaign to expel Iraq from Kuwait; market uncertainty resolves and supply fears ease.
Thai baht devaluation triggers a regional financial crisis across East Asia, sharply reducing oil demand forecasts.
OPEC fails to agree effective production cuts amid the Asian crisis; Brent falls to roughly $10/barrel, a two-decade low.
OPEC and non-OPEC producers (incl. Mexico, Norway) agree coordinated output cuts, beginning a sustained price recovery.
Attacks on the US trigger a global demand and confidence shock, briefly depressing oil prices amid recession fears.
The Iraq War begins, raising fears of prolonged supply disruption from a major OPEC producer.
Brent crude peaks amid strong global demand, dollar weakness, and speculative activity, just before the financial crisis hits.
Lehman Brothers' bankruptcy triggers a global financial crisis and demand collapse; oil prices crash from record highs.
Popular uprisings across the Middle East/North Africa, including civil war in Libya, disrupt regional oil production and exports.

Event: OPEC declines to cut output amid US shale boom
Description: OPEC (led by Saudi Arabia) maintains production despite oversupply from US shale, triggering a multi-month price collapse.
Nuclear-deal sanctions on Iran are lifted, allowing Iranian crude back onto world markets and adding to oversupply.
OPEC and non-OPEC producers (notably Russia) agree the first coordinated "OPEC+" output cut, supporting a price rally.

Event: US withdraws from Iran nuclear deal
Description: The US exits the JCPOA and re-imposes sanctions on Iranian oil exports, tightening expected global supply.
OPEC+ talks collapse; Saudi Arabia launches a price war and ramps output just as COVID-19 demand collapses.
OPEC+ agrees a record output cut to counter the COVID-19 demand collapse and negative-price panic in oil markets.
Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine raises fears of disruption to Russian energy exports, sending Brent above $100/barrel.
